title: Nursery Rhyme. CCXL. Charms.
content:
[A charm somewhat similar to the following may be seen in the 'Townley Mysteries,' p. 91. See a paper in the 'Arch�ologia,' vol. xxvii, p. 253, by the Rev. Lancelot Sharpe, M.A. See also MS. Lansd. 231, fol. 114, and Ady's 'Candle in the Dark,' 4to, London, 1650, p. 58.]

        Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John,
        Guard the bed that I lay on!
            Four corners to my bed,
            Four angels round my head;
        One to watch, one to pray,
        And two to bear my soul away!
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCXL. Charms." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
